name: CLAG-M (Cladribine + Cytarabine + G-CSF + Mitoxantrone)
goal: To achieve remission in relapsed/refractory AML or high-risk AML and bridge eligible patients to transplant.

chemo.name: Filgrastim (G-CSF)
notes: Continue until ANC ≥ 1000 for 2 consecutive days

chemo.name: Mitoxantrone
notes: Monitor cardiac function before starting therapy

chemo.name: Cytarabine (Ara-C)
notes: Start 2 hours after cladribine infusion finishes
High-dose cytarabine: monitor cerebellar signs before every dose

nursingTips: Monitor neurological status due to high-dose cytarabine.
TLS prophylaxis required: hydration + allopurinol ± rasburicase.
Strict infection monitoring; consider antimicrobial prophylaxis.
Central line recommended for all infusions.
references: NCCN AML Guidelines v.2024–2025 — 'Other Recommended Regimens' includes CLAG-M
MD Anderson Cancer Center CLAG-M protocol
Kadia TM et al., Salvage regimens in relapsed/refractory AML
KFSH&RC Hematology AML treatment pathways (internal clinical practice)
